ddls:
CREATE TABLE "universities" (
  "university_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each university */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the university */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the university is located */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the university is located */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of university (e.g., public, private) */,
  "tuition" TEXT /* Tuition fees */,
  "aid" TEXT /* Average financial aid */,
  "website" TEXT /* University website */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* University phone number */,
  "address" TEXT /* University address */,
  "accreditation_status" TEXT /* Accreditation status (e.g., accredited, not accredited) */,
  "ranking" TEXT /* University ranking (if available) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("university_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "admission_stats" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each admission stat */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university the stat belongs to */,
  "median_sat" TEXT /* Median SAT score */,
  "sat_range_25" TEXT /* 25th percentile SAT score */,
  "sat_range_75" TEXT /* 75th percentile SAT score */,
  "median_act" TEXT /* Median ACT score */,
  "act_range_25" TEXT /* 25th percentile ACT score */,
  "act_range_75" TEXT /* 75th percentile ACT score */,
  "average_gpa" TEXT /* Average GPA */,
  "admission_requirements" TEXT /* Admission requirements (e.g., letters of recommendation, essays) */,
  "application_deadline" TEXT /* Application deadline */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Notification date */,
  "acceptance_rate" TEXT /* Acceptance rate */,
  "graduation_rate" TEXT /* Graduation rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_admission_stats_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("university_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "programs" (
  "program_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each program */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university the program belongs to */,
  "program_name" TEXT /* Name of the program */,
  "degree_level" TEXT /* Degree level (e.g., undergraduate, graduate) */,
  "program_length" TEXT /* Program length (e.g., 4 years, 2 years) */,
  "program_description" TEXT /* Program description */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("program_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_programs_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("university_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, external user) */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date the user registered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the permission belongs to */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university the permission belongs to */,
  "permission_level" TEXT /* Permission level (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  "permission_expiration_date" TEXT /* Permission expiration date (if applicable) */,
  "permission_status" TEXT /* Permission status (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("university_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* ID of the university the review is about */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the review */,
  "review_rating" TEXT /* Rating given by the user (e.g., 1-5 stars) */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the review was written */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("university_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
